description: Draws a parabola on a 2D graph from a vertex point and focal parameter, or from an equation string. Vertex takes a point() expression or variable. Rotation uses the rotation() expression.
utility: Draw a parabola on a 2D graph by vertex+focal-distance or equation

arguments.name: graph
arguments.description: The 2D graph container to draw on

arguments.name: vertex
arguments.description: Vertex of the parabola as a point() expression or point variable. Defaults to origin if omitted

arguments.name: p
arguments.description: Focal parameter - distance from vertex to focus. Must be non-zero. Positive opens in rotation direction, negative opens opposite

arguments.name: rotation
arguments.description: Rotation angle using rotation() expression - e.g., rotation(pi/2). Default is 0 (opens right)

arguments.name: equation
arguments.description: Alternative to parametric args - equation string e.g. "y = x^2" or "x^2 = 4y"

alternates.description: Parabola at vertex with focal distance p
Parabola at origin with focal distance p

alternates.syntax: parabola(G, point(G, h, k), p, rotation(angle))

examples.expression:
G = g2d(at(2,2), 20, type(geometry))
parabola_1 = parabola(G, "y = x^2 - 4x + 3")

examples.scenario: Parabola at vertex with focal distance
examples.expression:
G = g2d(at(2,2), 20, type(geometry))
parabola_1 = parabola(G, point(G, 0, 0), 1)

G = g2d(at(2,2), 20, type(geometry))
V = point(G, 2, 3)
parabola_1 = parabola(G, V, 1.5)

G = g2d(at(2,2), 20, type(geometry))
parabola_1 = parabola(G, point(G, 0, 0), 1, rotation(pi/2))

examples.scenario: Sample point on parabola at ratio
Animate parallel ray reflection through focus
Plot parabola using function definition for ray-tracing through focus
examples.expression: G=g2d(at(3,11),size(23),range(-6,6),type(geometry)); f(x) = x^2/6 - 0.5; P = plot(G, f, -7, 7); F = point(G, 0, 1); H1=intersect(G,P,vline(G,point(G,-2,0)),defer()); l1=line(G, point(G, -2, 6), H1, defer()); l2=line(G, H1, F, type(segment), defer())
